lore:
The dock pass for Stowaway Step will grant you passage to the rest of the island, and the right to settle and build a life there. 

Many are under the impression that it grants through-passage to the Erenshor mainland, but alas, it does not.
A mysterious gem that tugs against something unseen
From somewhere deep beneath the waves.
A piece of Sivakaya has been captured in this Gem. Her presence can be felt - perhaps one day it could be used to summon her in her current form?
A tightly rolled note given to you by Percy with explicit instructions to hand it to the boatman who's preparing to depart from the docks.
A tiny replica of the one you talked to earlier.
Not good for much, but you could sell it.

Do not speak aloud of the forthcoming travel - there are ears everywhere and the mainland ports are closed. 

Remember, speak the word 'Hawthorne' to one of your brothers at the Ritual if you need assistance. You may present this note as evidence of my support should your brother doubt your motives

Do not delay!

-Omen Marrow

it fades out but ends in the line "... embrace of death."

and wave the moon farewell
it sinks beneath the skyline;
and morning breaks its spell.

...and so we reached for the forbidden fruit of the spire and its promises of power, and our god's wrath descended upon us like a torrent of flames. And we were buried beneath the ashes of our own destruction. We curse the name of our god, for in his fury, he sealed our fate and turned his back.

In the shadows of the wasteland where our city lies buried, whispers of vengeance echo among the remnants of our once-proud civilization. We, the survivors of divine wrath, have dared to dream of retribution against the god of fire and ice who condemned us to oblivion. 

With ancient knowledge and forbidden magic, we toil tirelessly to open a portal to the realm of the divine, seeking to confront our erstwhile deity and demand justice for our fallen kin.

Custodian,

The candle bears the light to rekindle memories lost to time. Shed it upon a shadow, and maybe it will illuminate something within the dark. We can find her still. She lives not within that husk that bears her image.

Our resplendent Goddess of War was not always veiled in shadow, nor did her heart once beat with the wicked cadence of necromantic sorcery. It is an unspoken truth, a silence that echoes through our ancient hallways.

The wisp's malevolent touch, like a ghostly hand upon the pages of our Codex, has cast aspersions upon the very essence of our goddess, obscuring the truths that lay concealed in the labyrinthine corridors of her past and then rewrote her motives...

...and then the wisp, after that tumultuous hour, severed ties with the goddess, their bond sundered by a schism that hinted at a greater, impending evil lurking in the void, an evil that drove Sivakaya to make her tragic choice to safeguard this world from an unfathomable calamity. 

The wisp's enigmatic departure stands as a testament to the complexities of divine sacrifice, and the shrouded lore that surrounds the goddess's descent from grace as she woefully embraced her new twisted and broken form.

The Wisp dares to defy the divine metamorphosis bestowed upon Sivakaya by Azynthi. Their misguided pursuits to strip her of the bestowed power and revert to a perceived former glory are treacherous. 

Sivakaya's transformation is no descent from grace but an ascension to a realm of dark magnificence, and all who fail to stand against those who seek to unravel this sublime tapestry woven by the shadows themselves shall fall to it.

Brax, once the beacon of power, now obscured in the fog of negligence. Fernalla, the tender nurturer, now tainted by indifference to the cries of her faithful. Vitheo, the arbiter of justice, has turned a blind eye to the righteous pleas of the downtrodden. Soluna, once the luminous guide, is veiled in apathy, forsaking the path of enlightenment. 
We cast our judgment upon these wayward gods, for their negligence and abandonment. For none have sacrificed as Sivakaya has.

In the wake of her transformation, the faithful of Sivakaya found themselves torn. Some held fast to the vision of her selfless sacrifice, a testament to her resolve to shield the world from ruin. 

Others were captivated by the dark power bestowed by Azynthi, heeding the call to arms that echoed in her name. Yet, beneath both of their fervors, an unseen dementia took root, shared with a subtle fracture in devotion as shadows twisted their hearts and blurred the lines of loyalty.

And so they parted ways—some drawn to the clamor of distant battles, others to the silence of candlelit halls, each seeking to honor her as they saw fit. 

Though no open rift split them, a quiet discord lingered, and in time, their paths diverged into unfamiliar silence—two fragments of a once-unified devotion, now strangers to one another but always united in the watch for Eldrin's deceitful hand.

She was equally lost to all, who were merely orphaned children, vying for her blessings. In misguided destruction or quiet meditation - her children wait.

As the faithful toiled in dark rooms by candlelight, even her steed decayed within the shadowed depths beneath sacred halls, holding vigil in the hollow stillness and the growing absence of hope.

And the Wisp's plots weave through the land, working to undo the beauty that has befallen Sivakaya

Yet there lingers a quiet hope, whispered among the faithful like the soft rustling of leaves in hidden groves. They speak of a return—a day when Sivakaya, untouched by the Wisp's grasp, will rise anew, her true form radiant with dark splendor and reunite her children.

Sireaethe has emerged from the shadows, claiming Sivakaya's presence within herself, and sharing her words with the faithful. The course is true, and all must remain steadfast in their resolve - for from beyond, she observes with approval.

Esteemed Orlok,


I trust this letter reaches you amidst the vigors of duty and loyalty to our cause. The time has come for a strategic move to safeguard the sanctity of Erenshor from the encroaching tendrils of Port Azure's influence.

Beneath the shadow of Sivakayan activity, let us begin.

-Eldoth

For the ritual, bones are key. Collect enough from the graves to stoke the flames for a day, and deliver them to the ruins. Do not delay, or else O.M. will be displeased.

One entry stands out: 

"Eldoth makes promises to reconnect us to our Goddess, yet never does he deliver. He delivers intel to us on Port Azure, however, his intel on Goodsoil was false. He is intensifying our campaign for his own benefit. If we are to take Azure, we cannot do it at his beck and whim. I have sent word north to the elders with my concerns."
Proof of a hard-earned victory over Azure Guard Captain Kilkay
Proof of a hard-earned victory over Ripper DeMerth
Erenshor's most prized export.

Behold, my loyal disciples, the whispers of the past have revealed a hidden tapestry woven beneath the desert's shifting veil! History herself beckons us to unearth the buried legacy of a forgotten civilization, a trove of secrets that shall eclipse the feeble ambitions of Port Azure and its covetous marauders. 

It is our sacred duty to defy these interlopers, to stand as guardians of the ancient mysteries that pulse with the divine essence coursing through this forsaken realm.

It is imperative that we remain in close proximity to the unfolding discoveries. Let the echoes of untainted history be our compass, and only when it becomes apparent that the Braxons are on the verge of unlocking the buried truths, shall you, my brethren, step forth. Interfere with the subtlety of shadows, for we must ensure that the power concealed within this ancient civilization is harnessed for our ascendancy alone.

The Wisp weaves its shadowy tendrils through the tapestry of this land, and only you, my trusted followers, are privy to its true purpose while the world rests peacefully on its bed of ignorance. 

Guard the secrets we unearth with utmost vigilance, for they hold the key to thwarting The Wisp's designs, lest we gain another obstacle to our endeavor.

The mystery of the sealed door to the forgotten catacomb looms like a specter. Here, a spirit's silent whispers weave tales of a lost candle, its value long obscured by the passage of time. 

Oh, how we mourn the absence of clarity, as we contemplate whether this elusive flame holds the key to unlocking the secrets hidden within the depths of the earth.

Blessed be we, chosen custodians of Erenshor, and as such must strive to ensure its wonder remains untainted by the grasping hands of politics and petty squabbles of men influenced by modernity

As such custodians, constant in our determination to prevail and bathed in promised wonder, we march forth.

Erenshor holds within its soul the very artifacts and treasures once used to construct our realm, steeped in turmoil of bygone eras, stained by the very essence of time itself, and so susceptible to the ruin of politics and the common man's hunger for power.

My friends, it is our burden to repel these threats.

So then arise, my companions, my friends, my soldiers. We, an indomitable legion of purity, can and might deliver prosperity to this plane.  

Let not the whispers of our intentions reach the ears of the uninitiated, for the power we seek is not for the unworthy.

Discretion is our armor, and fearlessness our blade.

We, the architects of destiny - the shepherds of our own legacy, cloaked in our armor and clutching our blades, driven by our determination to experience wonder - will bask in the light of a future only we may build. 

In blood shall we forge our legacy that transcends time - written in a language of ancient power and punctuated by the extraordinary sacrifices you may be asked to make.

We stand not just as seekers of mere trinkets, but as chosen custodians of the divine treasures hidden within this sacred land. The spirits of antiquity have anointed us with this privilege. 

Port Azure and its greedy marauders seek to plunder what is rightfully ours! It is your sacred duty to defy these interlopers. The land echoes with the cries of its untainted history, urging us to stand resolute.  Rise, my brethren, and let the divine essence that courses through this place fuel the flames of our conviction!

There stands upon this isle, a strange hermit - a recluse steeped in secrets, harboring knowledge that dances on the edge of forbidden. Azynthi's name is whispered in the same breath as his.

To confront him is to unravel the web that binds them both, yet caution whispers its own warnings, for there lies an untangible chill within him.

There is a reason why his name is uttered only in whispers. One should dare not do more.
It contains a unique energy, and you can sense it longing to be paired with something.
Its brilliance is stunning
It's freezing, but it never melts

Each day we pray for Soluna's return to free us from the beast that lurks beyond the canyon. The beast has crumbled and disguised itself as stardust, but it might reform at any moment... and I fear for our sanity in this place. 

Whatever the beast's reason for pursuing Soluna here, I hope that reason takes it away again soon. We are no match for it, and her guardians will not leave the shrine. 

We asked not for this gift. Our brothers dare not approach for fear of it being forced upon them too, and I worry our restoration is no longer possible.

Taking it into your hands allows its veil to leak around you, covering you.
A pungent leaf that smells like decay and leaves a rash.
The magical heart of a celestial dragon
Torn from the chest of a Vampire, it still beats from time to time.

With determination and focus, the world shifts, and what was once shrouded emerges in spectral light. Apparitions of truth drift through the air, casting faint glows on long-forgotten paths. All one must do is see.

The bridge is yours to cross.

Our necromancies diverge: mine trades blood for undying light; yours would restore light by bathing it in torment. Vitheo lies beyond my remedy, but you will pursue your own. 

I have given you the place and the way. In return, you will bring pressure to bear on Port Azure.  

May Sivakaya find the spark she seeks within Vitheo's husk - a nearer conduit to his ascended self, and thus to our reclamation.

-Eldoth

Born from an ignored wish... a tangible sadness can be felt from this wispy entity. Someone has given up all hope. This energy could weaken even the most optimistic forces.

Never before have I witnessed a curse so relentless! Ancient and unyielding, it condemns us to a cold, half-living state, our flesh turning to stone, and our minds slowly succumbing to madness. In fleeting moments of clarity, I try to write, but I sense those moments are growing ever shorter, fading into a darkness I can barely resist.

The fits of rage seem to ease when we are alone together, so we will seek refuge far from civilization, hoping to find a way to reverse the cold hardening of our bodies.


What could have caused this fate to befall us? Has Soluna awakened some unseen evil in the air?

The rage rises once more. Seclusion cannot keep it at bay forever, and death eludes us. I fear our downfall is inevitable.


Too often, I long to inflict the pain that consumes me.

Each day, I no longer pray for clarity or salvation from the rage.


Instead, I pray for an unsuspecting traveler to cross my path.


Tearing them apart brings a twisted joy.


Only then can I write. Only then do I feel relief.

The invader that looms threatens all we hold dear. Stand vigilant, for the hour of reckoning approaches, and our allegiance shall be tested.

Hold fast in the Swamp of Rottenfoot. Do not advance to Port Azure without further word. Our Lady Sivakaya, wrapped in her new cloak of dark, demands our loyalty. Await the courier for guidance.

In Faith of Azynthi,

-Darkwarden Ealdred

It recalls images of Vitheo's mortal form being laid to rest, surrounded by his most loyal followers. A notable gap between two men can be seen - someone is missing.

The missing person is not mourned, but hated. He has done something terrible. 

Balaste lays a hand on Vitheo's helmet, and the memory fades.

It recalls sorrow. A task has been given by a dying hero that cannot be declined. Her voice echoes as her spirit ascends, visibly battling a darkness with her own light. 

"Spare him of this", it cries. "If the odds say he will fall by his mother's twisted hand, fell him by your own - for she will never let go, not even in death."

"You must not share this wish - it must be you who carries the burden. Your mortal legacy will be tarnished, but your immortal legacy will be revered by the Gods..." and the voice fades away.

It recalls a glorious task - be granted a place of rest at the entrance of Vitheo's tomb, forever to guard its doors by presence alone. 

Memories of Vitheo's burial swirl, not clear, but still definite. 

Echoes of the Fernalla Plains Willow invade your mind, as though the memory originates from within the tree's very roots.

It recalls many emotions: Confusion, sorrow, and gratitude. Images of battle and betrayal swirl within. Images of his sister's face and voice, greeting him from a new and immortal place as he unwillingly leaves his mortal form. 

She is twisted and wrong, gripped by something that is familiar but vague. "I am so sorry," she whispers. 

A feeling of comfort, and the memory fades.
No matter how far you drift, one toss and you're pulled back to your anchor.
It's so sharp that you really can't not cut yourself on it when you handle it. Ow!
Inscribed faintly in the handle: "Eldrin"
It burns bright in your periphery, but when you look upon it, it turns to an impossibly black color.

A strange clump of dried  candle wax, breaking it open reveals a strange glow that quickly fades away. Visions sparkle across your eyes of a great Braxonian flame and hidden visages sharing truths. A strange word comes to mind... "Eldrin".

An old candle wick. Its tendrils flow in the breeze, and it whispers visions of shadows and tragedy into your mind as you hold it. As you study it you involuntarily mouth the word "Eldrin..."

Who is Eldrin?
Madness flows within where unending night terrors are held captive, safe from light.

To L. Kilfa

I hope this note finds you well. Our journey is set to begin tomorrow, and with any luck we'll arrive to see you within the week. 

Our training can begin at once. 

Until then, 
Bassalt Vallano

"My Dear Friend C. Pillot,

My concerns grow. Sivakayans have made the first move as we've all feared they would. If Fernalla remains able, she may be weighing options. This land cannot sustain a battle between Gods - we must seek the advice of The Wisp. We must make them see us. 

You have the key to their attention. It's time to bring it to existence."
A strange stone covered in what appears to be images of Brax and his Lover. It contains a small, coin shaped slot.

The faithful kneel before the flame, laying a stone paired with a token of his Lover, its surface warm with ancient memory. Beside it, a tool or token of their choosing—a silent request for favor.

One paired stone speaks a whisper. Two, a plea. Three, a chorus the Elder God cannot ignore. A request for favor is essential, but granting may not be.
Makes an absolutely awful tea, but people buy it.

It is imperative that the basement room in question is sealed with haste. 

It should be unaccessible to any who seek to enter. 

If the materials are not here on the island, use whatever you can until the proper materials arrive.

-E.M.

It's faded but legible:

"She yearns for the Willow. To cradle a single seed is to be graced; to cradle the seeds of all is to be sanctified. Such sanctification is seldom bestowed, for a Watcher may never abandon her vigil."

My dear sister, 

Find a reliable transit through the pass for our needs. We've established a foothold in the North away from the Molorai and other prying eyes. 

"Common thieves" is a term that will no longer apply to us if we can achieve our goal.

-Savannah

Precious Tiallia,

Taming the Malaroth is imperative to our cause. Imagine riding into the settle lands upon one of those beasts and the power that would warrant us.

Continue to capture and study them. Allow the tamers to feed them to attract their strongest, and observe their ways and weaknesses.

I am counting on you!

-Savannah

The giant rests until the flames are relit. When once again both burn bright we might rekindle its fury.

O', Vitheo has ascended and has cursed us for his worldly demise. On one glorious day we will invade his plane to end his influence once and for all.

The living flames must be relit upon fuels measured with precision. Do not guess, use the guide cards issued to all of our own.

Once both braziers burn again, the Giant will rise and we can access the Vithean Portal.

Full of tragedy and horror

Kilkay,

The North Villages are in ruin, their citizens have been slaughtered, only to be raised again by the Sivakayan demons to serve against our kind.

Your decision to withdraw your soldiers will be the most destructive in Erenshor's history for centuries to come.

We'll remain here to prevent the Sivakayans from decending on your city, however, know that once that threat is quelled, we plan to deal with you ourselves. 

Regards, Mavik Estonia, on behalf of Ripper

lore:
Finally, we have discovered the door to the ancients, but Evadne holds the only key. She will not be felled without first quelling the remnants of Brax's flames from which she feeds.

The stone I have procured from Orion Mycon for that purpose has bled away its power, as Orion was able to retain its band during our struggle.

Perhaps with Elderstone, we can forge another. Then we might be reunited with our lost ancestors once and for all.

It trembles as you hold it and its aroma smells like fear and nightmares. It clips handily onto your belt though, and just having it in your inventory will do well enough.

Together, the lovers created the land, and opened its gates to those who would come to settle - people of all faiths would come to be, and in peace they would live beneath the embrace of Siva and Brax - they would want for nothing and need for nothing, so it was decided.

To protect their paradise, Siva created a warrior unmatched by any who came before, and so not to encroach on the kingdom of Brax, she imbued him with the power of water and the speed of air. He was named Vitheo.

His oath to Sivakaya was sworn on the very altar upon which he was built, undying in its truth and unwavering in its tethers, it was forever done.

His contribution was not to security, but to sustenance and life. Brax created Fernalla, a demi-goddess in the image of Siva, she was born from life itself, and her fertility and pleasure spread throughout Erenshor causing the land to prosper with vibrance and joy.

Perfection had been achieved at the hand of the lovers, and together, they would be evermore.

We vow to steadfastly defend against Port Azure, ensuring your endeavors remain unimpeded.

As you ascend to divinity, may you reign as a powerful God.

We will continue otherwise as planned with the infiltrations.

Its ancient scrawling words are mostly illegible except for two sentences near the bottom:

"Only he who dons a pin shall be welcomed, blessed by Brax or by Sivakaya - it matters not, any Braxonian or Sivakayan historian might gift status to a pupil."

A boatman will be at the blight docks indefinitely, should any Sivakayan officers require passage. 

Present these documents.

It would be ill advised to travel to The Garden without purpose. There is no time for idle audience.

Only the Brazier within the coastal temple can ignite the portal.

With ancient coal for ignition, the most perfect carapace of a grass spider for a bowl to hold the meat of a wolf to summon his spirit, and a shard of Elderstone to enhance the flame, the gate may awaken.

I suspect I've uncovered the ingredients for the Fernallan Portal's ritual! I must obtain the remnants of a Daydream and a Nightmare from the Dusken Portal - a task that should prove simple with caution and blade. 

With those, it's simply a matter of procuring a dash of Faerie Dust and Aged Coal to ignite the flame.

We will stay the course and begin exports back to Merosavilla where armies will pay fortunes for such creatures. 

It is vital though, that in mixing the food we avoid adding the augmentation to the formula. It only invites disaster. Equal parts Octorunners, Clowny Fish, and some Azure Mead does the trick for attracting them. Use the Garden Shed oven.

I fear for our safety. I fear that our time is nearing its end. 

There have been cloaked figures lurking on the beach at night, after dark. I wonder if they know what knowledge I possess... and how they plan to extract it from me. I can only wonder what Eldoth has asked of them...

If you find this letter after my demise, see my Brother Yarren in Goodsoil. I've given something of great interest to him for safe keeping. Ask for him - he's often toiling away out of sight.

Nazura and Elwio and their abomination are sealed away in the Traitor's halls. May history forever look upon them in dismay. 

The resting Abomination clutches the key to Vitheo's resting place. I could not free it from his grasp, and I am confident no others will find success in that endeavor either.

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

The Bonepits... a place of great misery, not mystery. The rumors of riches here were quickly replaced by rusted metals and decaying gemstones, all seasoned by a great dose of undead stink.

I can cross this hole off my list and proceed on. The old ruins on the Vithean Watch coast hold some promise - I will go there next.

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

Krakengard proved interesting - an old Sivakayan fortress perhaps, its designs seemed to favor candlelight. 

It proved hopeless on clues though, and I shall continue my quest elsewhere. The Molorai troops are moving in, clearing the spiders and rats, and I am allowing them to evict me as well, as to avoid conflict with them. 

I will perhaps try my luck in Underspine Hollow.

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

When you think you've met the most dense being, it will be an ogre. Never before have I seen such brute-driven stupidity. 

A simple bit of concealing foliage, and I was free to roam. I unearthed nothing except maddening babbling and ill-located latrines. 

I've heard a rumor of an excavation under Port Azure... I will investigate there next.

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

Wow, just wow! It's amazing that there are hints of truth to the old stories about this place! The caverns gave way to old architecture and lost parts of the castle itself! 

Despite the spectral guards and overgrown insects, I managed to make my way through, and disappointingly, found nothing. 

I've caught wind of an event at the Elderstone Mines, perhaps my answers lie there.

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

The miners have cracked into something magnificent and terrifying, and slinking through the caverns was no easy feat. 

While many of the lizard men and the slimes carried noticable artifacts, none had that shared designs with the one which I seek. It is safe to eliminate this place from consideration.

I suppose I will head to Rockshade Hold and see if the rumors are true about that place.

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

It's tragic what descended upon the men of Rockshade Hold. They are doomed to walk unaging and cursed and - unfortunately - without even a hint of that which I seek. The stonemen can keep their hold, I am moving on to Abyssal Lake! 

I've heard rumors of a secret entrance!

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

Alas, I fear I have met my demise. Abyssal Lake was filled not with dumb beasts but stalking Sivakayans! 

They have sat me at a table with a chalice of wine and a hardy meal, and have interrogated me to no end for the secret of that which I seek. They will dispatch me from this life, I fear. I only hope to rest along the Western Sea Coast, for its sunsets are unmatched.

In decidedly feminine handwriting:

The Day Ages:
2pm brings snow-like seedlings drifting on the breeze
And then come more, carried with carefree ease
At 4, the sea thrashes, stretching high for the sky
A key to the Bellwain Estate's attic
A key to the Bellwain Estate's bathroom
A key to the Bellwain Estate's Study
A key to a Bellwain Estate Bedroom
A fine wine from Merosavilla, very aged and probably delicious.

Bellsie loved her detective novels. Loved to talk about how she could be an investigator and how good she'd be at it. Things like "hide in plain sight" and "they never look up, so hide things up high!"

She even went on a tangent about how the roof would be the best place to hide your most valuable items. Nobody ever robs a roof.
It's a pile of never-sent letters, seemingly written to Bellwain by Bellsie while he was away at sea. It feels wrong to read them.

Bellsie alwas said that the bathtub is in a dreadfully drafty spot. She said the walls held nothing back, and implored me to move the source to another room, however, it was architecturally impossible to do that.

Warming up by the fire after a bath is not the worst, after all.

Bellsie always preferred to relax on the back patio, though she worried greatly about the eroding retaining wall that held it all together. 

I had promised to have a swim around it to verify its integrity, but it wasn't until I was making preparations to leave that I noted the extent of its disrepair. 

Bellsie always was right.

In decidedly feminine handwriting:

The Day Ends
5pm brings the orange glow of the cooking fire
6pm, the sky burns gently with that same warmth and desire


Evenings spent laughing and love - forever and always together.

This is not a place to be alone. It is with age and sorrow that I leave tomorrow for one final voyage to the sea. I will not return to this place, for nothing holds me here. I've given back the materials, and now I give myself back to this world. 

I can only hope to be reunited with Bellsie one day, but alas, I do not deserve such a kind fate. 

Returning home can't always be the same gift that it was the day before.

Bellsie always carried her flint, now it's been confined to my safe - for losing it would be be removing the last of her from this place.

If only I could recall the beautiful array of colors she would create at the Angel Torches, it would be like being with her one last time... I can feel a strong presence of her in that place and I long for it back.

"The Garden Song"
By Bellsie Bellwain

I saw a vision in the petals' glow,
A passion stirred in blossoms below.
A hint of luck flows through the vine,
And now we flourish, intertwined!
The passion paid back with love,
A vision bloomed, the stars aligned.
Our luck was true, a fate designed.

(Written below)
It really does make the plants grow!

In decidedly feminine handwriting:

The Day Comes:
At 10am, the sky stretches into forever
Soon, red bugs arrive and flitter together
At noon, a plate of blue berries refreshes the soul
Then comes a rest on the soft, emerald grass below

In decidedly feminine handwriting:

Morning on the Isle:
At 7, the dew shines white, like fresh snow on the ground
At 8am, the blue sky lifts, waving softly to the sea
At 9, the green grass stirs awake, brushing gently in the breeze

Written in decidedly feminine handwriting:

Picnics do not get any better than some brackfruit-battered Island Fin!

-Island Fin Fish
-Brackfruit juice
-Bread
-1 Coal

Bellsie always came here to "wash off the smoke" after our drinks at the pergola campfire. 

Thing is, she always would forget her drink and made me come back to the bar to get it for her. I'd leave it on her chair if she was off in the gardens.

Truly a nuisance in the moment, but a task I miss dearly now.

Our favorite wine was...  illicit. It's hard to source, and so I've resorted to keeping a stash in a nook beneath the docks. It's not the most accessible but its safe from prying eyes. 

I could not fathom running out, Bellsie would be heartbroken too. 

I had never planned to sail again, unless it was for our wine.
A key to the Bellwain Estate's Study

Bellsie always said the Sun Lilies bloomed at 6pm, but I've never seen them in the garden, even though she sang her song there each day for the plants.

I will not forget her voice, I wish I could remember the words.

Should any set  foot in this home, I implore you to return from where you came. It holds loss and sorrow and hopelessness - all of the wealth i have sailed for is worthless alone. 

My love, my light, my Bellsie is no more, and so shall I return to the sea one last time. 

I have narrated our lives here if you are inclined to stay, please honor our memories. 

-Bellwain

It vibrates in your hands, and calls out a name: "Posta..."
A thought twists within your mind as you hold it: "Revid..."
A swirling entity dances about inside. It cries a name from time to time: "Stalzo..."
